category_title: Disengaged adolescent with behavioral history and word-skipping habits
student_description: Derek is a disengaged student who masks his reading struggles by rushing through texts, skipping words and lines just to finish tasks quickly. He exhibits defensive body language and apathy, requiring relational trust-building and specific accommodations like having texts read aloud to bypass his visual tracking issues and ensure comprehension.

criteria.rationale: Derek's habit of skipping words to finish quickly means independent reading fails; he needs the text read to him to ensure comprehension.
criteria.check: The tutor reads the text aloud to the student or provides an auditory alternative, explicitly avoiding requiring the student to read the text independently.

criteria.rationale: Breaking reading into short, modeled segments prevents Derek from feeling overwhelmed and rushing to the end of a page just to be done.
criteria.check: The tutor breaks the reading or task into short, manageable segments and models the process, rather than assigning a large block of text at once.
